title: Introductory Python Programming Exam
project: P09 Adaptive AI Tutor for Programming Concepts
instructions: Write Python code for each function or class exactly as named.
Return values from functions instead of printing them unless the prompt explicitly says otherwise.
Visible test cases are examples; hidden tests may check additional edge cases.

questions.title: Weighted Score
questions.prompt: Write a function weighted_score(coursework, exam) that returns the final score. Coursework is worth 40% and exam is worth 60%.
hints: Multiply each score by its weight.
Add the two weighted values and return the result.
questions.solution:
def weighted_score(coursework, exam):
    return coursework * 0.4 + exam * 0.6

questions.title: Ticket Price
questions.prompt: Write a function ticket_price(age) that returns 0 for ages under 5, 8 for ages 5 to 17, 12 for ages 18 to 64, and 6 for ages 65 or older.
hints: Check the smallest age range first.
Use if and elif to separate the ranges.

questions.title: Count Short Words
questions.prompt: Write a function count_short_words(words, max_length) that returns how many strings in words have length less than or equal to max_length.
hints: Start a counter at 0.
Loop through the list and compare len(word) with max_length.

questions.title: Clean Username
questions.prompt: Write a function clean_username(username) that removes leading and trailing spaces and returns the username in lowercase.
hints: Use strip() to remove outside spaces.
Use lower() to make the text lowercase.

questions.title: Numbers Divisible By
questions.prompt: Write a function divisible_by(numbers, divisor) that returns a new list containing only the numbers divisible by divisor. Assume divisor is not 0.
hints: Use the modulo operator.
Append a number when number % divisor equals 0.

questions.title: Password Strength
questions.prompt: Write a function password_strength(password) that returns 'weak' if the password has fewer than 8 characters, 'medium' if it has at least 8 characters but no digit, and 'strong' if it has at least 8 characters and at least one digit.
hints: Check the length first.
Use a loop or any() to check whether a character is a digit.

questions.title: Top Student
questions.prompt: Write a function top_student(records) where records is a list of dictionaries with 'name' and 'score'. Return the name of the student with the highest score. If records is empty, return None. If there is a tie, return the first matching student.
hints: Handle the empty list first.
Track the current best record while looping.

questions.title: Character Frequency
questions.prompt: Write a function char_frequency(text) that returns a dictionary counting each non-space character in lowercase.
hints: Convert the text to lowercase.
Skip characters equal to a space.

questions.title: Matrix Diagonal Sum
questions.prompt: Write a function diagonal_sum(matrix) that returns the sum of the main diagonal of a square matrix.
hints: The diagonal uses positions [0][0], [1][1], [2][2], and so on.
Loop over the row index.

questions.title: Course Gradebook
questions.prompt: Create a Gradebook class. It should store scores in a list. Add add_score(score), average(), and highest() methods. average() should return None if there are no scores. highest() should return None if there are no scores.
hints: Initialize self.scores as an empty list in __init__.
Use sum and len for average, and max for highest.

questions.title: Balanced Parentheses
questions.prompt: Write a function is_balanced_parentheses(text) that returns True if every opening parenthesis '(' is closed by a later ')' in the correct order. Ignore all non-parenthesis characters.
hints: You can use a counter instead of a full stack because there is only one bracket type.
The counter should never become negative.

questions.title: Recommend Weakest Exercise
questions.prompt: Write a function recommend_weakest_exercise(mastery, exercises) that returns the id of the first exercise targeting the student's weakest KC. mastery maps KC ids to scores from 0 to 1. exercises is a list of dictionaries with 'id' and 'kc_tags'. If mastery or exercises is empty, return None. If multiple KCs have the same lowest mastery, use the one that appears first when iterating through mastery.
hints: Find the weakest KC by looping through mastery in insertion order.
Then return the first exercise whose kc_tags contains that KC.
